title: Berenberg initiates Vulcan Materials stock with Hold rating
summary: Berenberg initiated coverage on Vulcan Materials Company (NYSE:VMC) with a Hold rating and a $283.00 price target, closely aligning with its current trading price of $281.56. The firm highlighted Vulcan Materials' strong position in the U.S. aggregates market, benefiting from robust demand, pricing power, and potential acquisition growth. Despite a perfect Piotroski Score of 9, InvestingPro suggests the stock may be overvalued, even as the company reported strong Q1 2026 financial results and declared a quarterly dividend.

title: ASGN Inc stock (US04621X1081): focus on recent earnings trends after ticker change to Everforth
summary: The article discusses the transition of ASGN Inc to Everforth (EFOR) on the NYSE, highlighting its focus on IT services, technology consulting, and digital solutions. It emphasizes the importance of tracking recent quarterly results and the impact of the rebranding on the company's market positioning and future performance, especially within U.S. federal and commercial sectors. The rebranded company continues to be a key player in the technology spending landscape, with its earnings closely tied to project volumes and demand for advanced tech skills.

title: International Business Machines Corp (IBM) Stock Up 7.6% but GF Value Says Overvalued -- GF Score: 77/100
summary: International Business Machines Corp (IBM) stock rose 7.6% to $320.42, but GuruFocus's GF Value™ indicates it is 34.6% overvalued compared to its intrinsic value of $238.11. Despite a GF Score™ of 77/100, suggesting potential for long-term returns, concerns remain regarding its financial strength (5/10) and momentum (4/10). The current P/E ratio of 28.3x is also 17% above its 5-year median, further reinforcing the overvaluation assessment.

title: Shinhan, KB, Toss Hold Closed-Door Talks on Won-Backed Stablecoin
summary: Shinhan Financial Group, KB Financial Group, Toss, and several regional banks recently held a closed-door meeting to discuss their response to won-backed stablecoin legislation and the evolving digital asset market. This move indicates a growing urgency among financial institutions to form alliances, especially as Hana Financial Group rapidly advances its partnerships with entities like Dunamu and Naver to establish a first-mover advantage in the expanding on-chain financial ecosystem. Securities firms are also actively acquiring stakes in cryptocurrency exchanges, further accelerating the need for banks to collaborate to secure platforms, exchanges, and payment networks in the nascent stablecoin market.
